excerpt: 1989 Grozny population: 397,000.
transform_note: Used as prewar baseline anchor only, not as episode denominator by itself.
confidence_note: Baseline demographic figure, not a wartime presence count.

excerpt: HRW estimated 100,000 civilians remained in Grozny.
transform_note: Mid-episode denominator anchor.
confidence_note: Snapshot, not a continuous time series.

excerpt: Mid-January benchmark implies ~100,000 remained.
transform_note: Triangulates the HRW 100,000 figure.
confidence_note: Derived directly from the source's fraction and rounded city baseline.

excerpt: Early-March remainder estimate: ~30,000.
transform_note: Used as late-episode / immediate post-episode tail anchor.
confidence_note: Ethnic composition note suggests the city population that remained was selective, not representative.

excerpt: 23,700 men assembled for the intervention on 11 Dec.
transform_note: Used as lower/starting federal-force envelope; not all were necessarily in Grozny at once.
confidence_note: Whole task force, not a city-only count.

excerpt: 38,000 Russian forces were assembled near Grozny by 31 Dec.
transform_note: Upper federal-force envelope for direct assignment around the city.
confidence_note: Near-Grozny total, not all in the city proper.

excerpt: RAND gives 6,000 for the New Year's Eve penetrating force.
transform_note: In-city assault component, not whole cordon.
confidence_note: Specific assault echelon, useful as city-proper lower anchor.

excerpt: Chechen defender estimates range from roughly 1,000 to 10,000.
transform_note: Very broad rebel-force envelope; used only as sensitivity context, not as a point estimate.
confidence_note: Extremely uncertain and source-dependent.

excerpt: City-proper envelope: 4,000-5,000 Russian troops and 2,000-2,500 enemy.
transform_note: Summed to a city-proper combatant exposure anchor.
confidence_note: Reflects city-proper counts, not the whole siege ring.

excerpt: Thomas contrasts 5,000 in the city with a notional requirement of 50,000-60,000.
transform_note: Used as a warning against understating the broader assault/cordon force requirement.
confidence_note: The 50,000-60,000 figure is a doctrinal requirement, not an observed deployment.

excerpt: Commission estimate: 1,376 killed and 408 missing.
transform_note: Used as a documented federal-death floor; broader than the exact Grozny episode and excludes Chechen combatants.
confidence_note: Operation-wide federal figure, not city-only.

excerpt: High-end estimate: 5,000-6,000 Russian killed.
transform_note: Used only as an upper federal-loss envelope, not directly as the city estimate.
confidence_note: Very uncertain and broader than the target episode.

excerpt: Felgenhauer gives ~1,000 dead in the battle and 1,500 official dead by mid-February.
transform_note: Closer to the Grozny episode than the broader House of Commons figure.
confidence_note: Federal-only and retrospective.

excerpt: Kovalev-associated group reported 25,000 civilians known or believed killed.
transform_note: Used as the high side of the Grozny civilian-death range, acknowledging some 'believed' cases.
confidence_note: Likely centered on Grozny/early campaign but not perfectly clipped to city dates.

excerpt: Alternative observer estimate: around 15,000 civilians killed.
transform_note: Used as the low side of the Grozny civilian-death range.
confidence_note: Broader than a pure city-only event count, but still squarely in the Grozny assault period.

excerpt: Amnesty cites ~27,000 war-related deaths in Grozny alone.
transform_note: Retained as contextual upper-tail evidence but excluded from the core direct-death path because the wording is 'war-related deaths', not a clean direct/status-specified series.
confidence_note: Ambiguous as to civilian/combatant composition and direct vs. indirect components.

excerpt: City hospital data: 55 local residents killed, 186 injured.
transform_note: Candidate episode anchor only; no full DR estimate completed.
confidence_note: Strong numerator anchor but not yet paired with exposure denominators.

excerpt: Same hospital report gives 186 injured.
transform_note: Auxiliary injury context for the proposed Shali episode.
confidence_note: Hospital-based and likely undercounts those not reaching care.

excerpt: Early-April village population-present estimate: 4,500 to 6,000.
transform_note: Core civilian denominator source for the Samashki episode.
confidence_note: Best single denominator source in the bundle.

excerpt: Pre-assault reporting put armed supporters in the 200-400 range.
transform_note: Used only as an outer sensitivity envelope; later reports suggest far fewer remained during the assault.
confidence_note: One month earlier than the assault and likely an overstatement for 7-8 April.

excerpt: Reported federal dead ranged from 0 to 19.
transform_note: Used as a federal-loss component in the Samashki combatant-death range.
confidence_note: Short operational brief; range itself is explicitly contradictory.

excerpt: Named-victim list includes 13 women.
transform_note: Treated as a strong civilian proxy subgroup, following the project rules.
confidence_note: Named-victim floor; some additional unlisted female deaths are possible.

excerpt: Named-victim list records 7 victims aged 18 and younger.
transform_note: Exact source-native threshold retained; not silently harmonized to u15.
confidence_note: Named-victim floor.

excerpt: Memorial identified 4 battle deaths and up to 10 more possibly battle-related.
transform_note: Used as the low floor and a modest upper sub-envelope for local armed fatalities.
confidence_note: These deaths are from the village side only and do not include federal losses.

excerpt: Parliamentary-commission channel cites about 60 pro-Dudayev losses.
transform_note: Used as the conservative upper combatant-death bound rather than the higher official press line.
confidence_note: Still vulnerable to status inflation, but less extreme than the official 120-130 claim.

excerpt: Official MVD line claimed 120-130 fighters killed.
transform_note: Retained as a disputed official claim but excluded from the core estimate because the source itself says authorities 'wrote them all off as fighters.'
confidence_note: Very likely inflated by status reclassification of male villagers.

excerpt: HRW annual report states 103 civilians were killed.
transform_note: Low anchor for the Samashki civilian-death estimate.
confidence_note: Concise retrospective statement, likely based on Memorial's named-victim work.

excerpt: HRW's contemporaneous summary gives a 100-200 civilian range.
transform_note: Used as the high side of the core civilian estimate path.
confidence_note: Broad range rather than a named-victim list.

excerpt: UN summary corroborates at least 103 civilian deaths.
transform_note: Corroborative low anchor.
confidence_note: UN is summarizing NGO findings rather than independently recounting bodies.

excerpt: Amnesty gives a high-end allegation of >250 civilian deaths.
transform_note: Kept as contextual upper-tail evidence and not used in the core estimate because the underlying audit trail is not transparent here.
confidence_note: May incorporate additional unverified or delayed deaths through the access-denial period.

excerpt: Retrospective Memorial summary restates 103 victims and only four known armed.
transform_note: Corroborative retrospective restatement of the Memorial findings.
confidence_note: Retrospective summary, not a fresh field count.

excerpt: Felgenhauer's August 1996 snapshot: 5,000 rebels against a 10,000 MVD garrison.
transform_note: Candidate force anchor for a future August 1996 DR estimate.
confidence_note: Good episode-separation evidence but not enough for a full estimate here.
